text:
Once upon a time, in a small village nestled between rolling hills and dense forests, a wise old owl named Oliver lived in a towering oak tree. He had seen many generations come and go and had countless tales to tell.

One rainy afternoon, Oliver was sitting on a crooked branch, listening to the soft patter of rain against his feathers. A young squirrel named Hazel bounced up and down.

"Haven't heard your favorite story yet?" asked Hazel.

"About my dear wife?" Oliver sighed fondly.

"It's magnificent! You tell it every spring."

Their favorite story was about a mermaid princess who fell in love with an adventurous human boy. Every year, she'd send him a golden feather. One year, he found a pearl so big it could fit a whole owl nest. They lived happily ever after.

"You always say that," Hazel chuckled.

"The only thing I regret is not spending enough time with my two dear friends."
